steps.title: Represent the starting counts
steps.content: Let the starting number of each species be $N$. The starting counts are equal, so the same variable can represent both.

steps.title: Find the final counts
steps.content: The prey increased by $2{,}300\%$ of $N$, so its final count is $N+23N=24N$. The predators increased by $200\%$ of $N$, so their final count is $N+2N=3N$.

steps.title: Convert the comparison
steps.content: The prey count is $$ \frac{24N}{3N}=8 $$ times the predator count. Being $8$ times as large means it is $7$ times greater: $$ 7(100\%)=700\% $$ The answer is 700.
